original_hypothesis.title: NukeFund receive() can be griefed via forced ETH (selfdestruct) causing fund/accounting divergence and payout anomalies
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: NukeFund tracks an internal 'fund' that only increases inside receive(). ETH forced into the contract via selfdestruct (or other balance-changing mechanisms that bypass receive()) increases address(NukeFund).balance without increasing fund. This can create confusing solvency/accounting states and distort expected payout ratios (payouts use 'fund' not actual balance), potentially trapping ETH permanently or enabling governance disputes over unaccounted funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a common operational risk: attackers can force ETH into contracts. Here it creates a durable mismatch between actual balance and claim pool accounting, which may surface as user-facing discrepancies and complicate incident response.

proof_of_signal.pattern: fund is a private accounting var updated only in receive(); nuke payout uses fund, not address(this).balance

preconditions: Attacker can deploy a contract that selfdestructs to NukeFund address (permissionless)
Protocol/UI/users interpret NukeFund.balance as claimable or expect all ETH in contract to be in 'fund'
No sweep/reconcile function exists to incorporate stray ETH into fund

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Internal accounting not reconciled with actual ETH balance

operational_questions: Do any analytics/UIs display address(NukeFund).balance as available fund instead of getFundBalance()?
Is there an intended mechanism to reconcile stray ETH into fund (none in code shown)?
Could this mismatch cause governance disputes or mispriced nuking strategies?
validation_requirements: Force-send ETH to NukeFund in tests and confirm fund remains unchanged
Assess whether nuking ever becomes impossible despite surplus balance (should not), and whether surplus becomes stranded

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Force ETH into NukeFund via selfdestruct
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Observe discrepancy between getFundBalance() and address(NukeFund).balance
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Use discrepancy to create user confusion / reputational harm (or exploit any off-chain accounting that assumes equality)

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Accounting inconsistency; potential stranded ETH; user confusion about claim pool size
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium; requires new function or off-chain reconciliation policy

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users report 'missing' ETH not reflected in fund

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
